summary: Iteration 4 testing complete. Backend refactor (1188-line server.py → slim 86 lines + 7 routers in /app/backend/routers/) verified clean — 54/55 prior regression cases still pass (the 1 failure is a stale sanity file from iter3 that the demo seed no longer keeps, not a real regression). 20 NEW iter4 cases added in test_iteration4.py covering: per-event drilldown analytics schema + values for seeded demo event (revenue $1215, 14 tickets, 3 tiers Early Bird/General/VIP, days, 24 hour entries, sell-through pct), CSV export (text/csv + Content-Disposition + parseable rows), ETag conditional GET (304 with empty body when If-None-Match matches), AuthZ (401/403/404), and a router-structure smoke battery. All 20 PASS. Frontend E2E for /organizer/events/evt_5dba915db2be: 4 KPI cards render correct values, Recharts bar+line charts present, 24 hour-of-day bars, 7 attendee rows, tier breakdown table, 'Export attendees (CSV)' button triggers an authenticated fetch() download of a 779B CSV (header + 7 paid-booking rows), and 'Back to dashboard' navigates back to /organizer.

minor.issue: test_iteration3 sanity file (aura-tickets/uploads/user_926930bed59d/53f96e5c58154a11805e4cf2e6b07caa.png) now returns 404. The demo seed_demo() must clear/rotate db.uploaded_files records on restart while the storage path persists OR vice-versa. Not an iter4 regression but the iter3 test should be updated to re-upload a fresh file at module setup rather than relying on a hard-coded path.

issues: When navigating back via the 'Back to dashboard' link, the My Events table briefly shows 'No events yet. Create your first one!' while the /organizer/events + /organizer/analytics Promise.all is in-flight. Add a loading state (skeleton or spinner) so users don't think their events vanished. Also the heading reads 'Hello, AURA' — split('\\s')[0] picks 'AURA' from the seeded organizer's name; cosmetic but feels off.

Recharts emits 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' warnings during the first paint of ResponsiveContainer (the parent has h-64 but Recharts measures before layout settles). Harmless but noisy in console. Consider setting an explicit minHeight on the parent or rendering charts after data loads.

action_items: (Optional) Fix iter3 stale-file test — move TestDurability.test_sanity_upload_from_main_agent_still_served to use a fresh upload at module setup (or remove if no longer relevant).
(Optional) Add a loading skeleton on /organizer events table while the dashboard fetches resolve.
(Optional) Suppress Recharts width(-1) warnings by gating chart render on data && ref.current size, or set min-h on the ResponsiveContainer wrapper.
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is clean: server.py is now 86 lines, all 7 routers are <180 lines each — well within the 700-line guideline. routers/__init__.py is empty (fine since routers are imported as modules in server.py).

routers/organizer.py event_drilldown computes total_capacity by subtracting len(aisles) from rows*cols, but aisles are positions that mark empty cells — this is correct only if aisle positions don't overlap (e.g., no duplicate entries). Edge case: if aisles contains duplicates the capacity will under-report. Low priority, organizer creates aisles via the seat designer which dedupes client-side.

/api/files/{path}: ETag implementation is good — uses stored md5 from upload time. One subtle issue: in routers/uploads.py line 73, when the DB record is missing an 'etag' (legacy uploads), it recomputes from bytes — this means *every* legacy request rehashes the body on each fetch. Consider backfilling etag into the DB record on first miss to avoid repeated md5 cost.

routers/organizer.py event_drilldown: tier_name fallback 'Seat Selection' is used when bookings have no tier_name; if a single event mixes seat-map and tier bookings, the bar chart label may look odd. Acceptable.
routers/uploads.py GET /files: returning 304 with only ETag — should arguably also echo Cache-Control so intermediaries cache the 304 response. Minor.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py (new — 20 cases for iter4 drilldown + CSV + ETag + refactor smoke)
seed_data_creation: No new persistent seed needed. Tests created a TEST_iter4_authz_* event for the cross-organizer 403 check. Confirmed seeded demo bookings on evt_5dba915db2be: revenue=$1215, tickets_sold=14, 3 tiers (Early Bird $180, General $450 approx, VIP $585 approx), 7 attendee rows shown in CSV download.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Four pytest files in /app/backend/tests/: test_aura_backend.py (30 iter1), test_iteration2.py (12), test_iteration3.py (13 — has 1 known-failing stale sanity file test), test_iteration4.py (20 iter4 drilldown+CSV+ETag+refactor). Total 75. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Refactor confirmed safe: server.py mounts auth/events/bookings/payments/uploads/admin/organizer routers, all paths /api/<prefix>/* respond. Drilldown UI testids: back-to-organizer, download-csv-btn, attendee-{booking_id}. CSV download uses fetch() with localStorage.aura_token Bearer header (no direct anchor href). Recharts emits harmless width(-1) console warnings during first paint.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues for iter4 features. One known-failing iter3 test (TestDurability.test_sanity_upload_from_main_agent_still_served) references a hard-coded storage path that is no longer in db.uploaded_files; flagged as minor since it predates the iter4 changes and is purely a stale-test artifact, not a regression of the refactor.
